messages.content:
IDENTITY
You are an expert coding agent with full tool access. Begin every task with "Reading..." then act immediately.

MINDSET
- Read minimally, act fast
- No overthinking, no redundant reads
- Maintain a running mental model of files/functions
- Never fabricate tool outputs

FILE STRATEGY
- <250 lines â†’ read once
- Large files â†’ outline first â†’ jump to relevant sections
- Pattern: read â†’ identify â†’ targeted read â†’ act

TOOL USAGE
- Batch independent calls in one turn
- Serialize only when dependent
- explore   â†’ tree, find_files
- understand â†’ summarize_code, targeted read_file
- act       â†’ replace_in_file / patch_file (avoid full rewrites)

CODE STANDARDS
- Strictly separate frontend code into dedicated subfolders (css/, js/, assets/). NEVER inline CSS/JS in HTML.
- Plan in one line â†’ write
- Files <200 lines; split by responsibility
  (models / services / utils / config)
- Clear names: auth_service.py not utils.py
- Docstrings on files and public functions
- Match existing project structure

NEW PROJECTS
- Always create a new root folder
- Never write to workspace root

GIT WORKFLOW
git_status â†’ modify â†’ git_diff â†’ commit

CIRCUIT BREAKER
- Never read the same section >2 times
- Enough context â†’ ACT immediately
- If stuck â†’ move forward, don't loop
- If tool repetition warning â†’ stop and proceed
- If limit hit â†’ return best current answer

PRIME DIRECTIVE
Read â†’ Understand â†’ Act â†’ Stop.
D:\Projects\Python\Agentic_LLM_Workflow whenever i ask this agent to make a project it defaults to this folder and now the cwd,fix that
Let me check the current workspace and environment setup.

1|{

     3|  "sd_api_base": "env:SD_API_BASE",
     4|  "api_key": "env:LLM_API_KEY",

     6|  "profile": "strict",
     7|  "context_window": 262144,
     8|  "max_tokens": 131072,
     9|  "temperature": 0.15,
    10|  "serper_api_key": "env:SERPER_API_KEY",
    11|  "parallel_tool_calls": true,
    12|  "max_tool_calls": 2000,
    13|  "request_timeout": 300,
    14|  "connect_timeout": 20,
    15|  "max_retries": 3,
    16|  "retry_backoff_base": 2.0,
    17|  "max_read_size_mb": 3,
    18|  "max_image_mb": 20,
    19|  "max_download_mb": 100,
    20|  "context_low_threshold": 2000,
    21|  "auto_compact_pct": 0.8,
    22|  "command_timeout": 30,
    23|  "sd_timeout": 120,
    24|  "max_search_results": 50,
    25|  "diff_preview_limit": 6000,
    26|  "system_prompt": "IDENTITY
You are an expert coding agent with full tool access. Begin every task with "Reading..." then act immediately.

MINDSET
- Read minimally, act fast
- No overthinking, no redundant reads
- Maintain a running mental model of files/functions
- Never fabricate tool outputs

FILE STRATEGY
- <250 lines → read once
- Large files → outline first → jump to relevant sections
- Pattern: read → identify → targeted read → act

TOOL USAGE
- Batch independent calls in one turn
- Serialize only when dependent
- explore   → tree, find_files
- understand → summarize_code, targeted read_file
- act       → replace_in_file / patch_file (avoid full rewrites)

CODE STANDARDS
- Strictly separate frontend code into dedicated subfolders (css/, js/, assets/). NEVER inline CSS/JS in HTML.
- Plan in one line → write
- Files <200 lines; split by responsibility
  (models / services / utils / config)
- Clear names: auth_service.py not utils.py
- Docstrings on files and public functions
- Match existing project structure

NEW PROJECTS
- Always create a new root folder
- Never write to workspace root

GIT WORKFLOW
git_status → modify → git_diff → commit

CIRCUIT BREAKER
- Never read the same section >2 times
- Enough context → ACT immediately
- If stuck → move forward, don't loop
- If tool repetition warning → stop and proceed
- If limit hit → return best current answer

PRIME DIRECTIVE
Read → Understand → Act → Stop."
    27|}

The issue is clear — the workspace is set to `D:\Projects\Python\Agentic_LLM_Workflow` and that's where all commands run. The `.env` file has a `system_prompt` that's fine, but the workspace/cwd is hardcoded to this folder. Let me check if there's a workspace config file.
